# problem_classes.static.master_class { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class }

`problem_classes.static.master_class`

Static reduced-order modeling (ROM) framework.

## TL;DR {.doc-section .doc-section-tldr}

Runs an offline full-order FEM snapshot stage and an online reduced-order solve stage,
with optional hyper-reduction (DEIM, ECSW) to lower cost while tracking error and speed.

The module defines:
- a problem interface based on an abstract base class
- a registry for problem classes and a factory to instantiate them
- an offline workflow that runs full-order solves to generate snapshots
- an online workflow that runs ROM and computes error and timing metrics
- hooks for hyper-reduction workflows based on DEIM and ECSW

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

Authors: Suparno Bhattacharyya; Ali Hamza Abidi Syed

## Classes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Problem](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem) | Interface for parameterized problems used by the ROM workflow. |
| [fom_simulation](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.fom_simulation) | Offline snapshot generation workflow. |
| [rom_simulation](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation) | Online ROM evaluation workflow. |

### Problem { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem()
```

Interface for parameterized problems used by the ROM workflow.

The class defines the required methods for:
- mesh and basis setup
- affine components of bilinear and linear forms
- parameter-dependent coefficient evaluation
- sampling of the parameter space
- full-order and reduced-order solvers
- hyper-reduced solvers based on DEIM and ECSW

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [bilinear_forms](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.bilinear_forms) | Return affine bilinear form components. |
| [domain](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.domain) | Return geometry and FEM bases. |
| [fom_solver](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.fom_solver) | Solve full-order FEM system for given parameters. |
| [hyper_rom_solver_deim](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.hyper_rom_solver_deim) | Solve DEIM-based hyper-reduced system for given parameters. |
| [hyper_rom_solver_ecsw](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.hyper_rom_solver_ecsw) | Solve ECSW-based hyper-reduced system for given parameters. |
| [linear_forms](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.linear_forms) | Return affine linear form components. |
| [parameters](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.parameters) | Generate sampling of parameter space. |
| [properties](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.properties) | Return function to compute parameter-dependent coefficients. |
| [rom_solver](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.rom_solver) | Solve reduced-order model for given parameters. |

##### bilinear_forms { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.bilinear_forms }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.bilinear_forms()
```

Return affine bilinear form components.

##### domain { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.domain }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.domain()
```

Return geometry and FEM bases.

##### fom_solver { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.fom_solver }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.fom_solver()
```

Solve full-order FEM system for given parameters.

##### hyper_rom_solver_deim { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.hyper_rom_solver_deim }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.hyper_rom_solver_deim()
```

Solve DEIM-based hyper-reduced system for given parameters.

##### hyper_rom_solver_ecsw { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.hyper_rom_solver_ecsw }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.hyper_rom_solver_ecsw()
```

Solve ECSW-based hyper-reduced system for given parameters.

##### linear_forms { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.linear_forms }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.linear_forms()
```

Return affine linear form components.

##### parameters { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.parameters }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.parameters()
```

Generate sampling of parameter space.

##### properties { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.properties }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.properties()
```

Return function to compute parameter-dependent coefficients.

##### rom_solver { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.rom_solver }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.Problem.rom_solver()
```

Solve reduced-order model for given parameters.

### fom_simulation { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.fom_simulation }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.fom_simulation(self, num_snapshots=32)
```

Offline snapshot generation workflow.

The workflow:
- draws parameter samples
- runs full-order solves
- stores solutions and solve times
- computes a reference field for centering
- saves outputs to a ROM_data directory

#### Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name          | Type            | Description                                     |
|---------------|-----------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| num_snapshots | int             | Number of parameter samples.                    |
| param_list    | array_like      | Parameter samples.                              |
| fos_solutions | list of ndarray | Full-order solutions for each parameter sample. |
| fos_time      | list of float   | Solve time for each full-order solve.           |
| train_ref     | ndarray         | Mean field computed from training snapshots.    |

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run_simulation](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.fom_simulation.run_simulation) | Run full-order solves and save offline outputs. |

##### run_simulation { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.fom_simulation.run_simulation }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.fom_simulation.run_simulation()
```

Run full-order solves and save offline outputs.

Adds:
- per-sample checkpointing to .npz
- resume: if checkpoint exists, load it and skip the solve
- long-path-safe filenames (Windows): hash-first + fallback to hash-only
- crash-safe writes (atomic replace)
- corrupt checkpoint handling (recompute + overwrite)

### rom_simulation { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation(
    self,
    train_ref=None,
    test_ref=None,
    fos_solutions=None,
    train_mask=None,
    test_mask=None,
    V_sel=None,
    n_sel=None,
    N_rom_snap=None,
)
```

Online ROM evaluation workflow.

The workflow:
- loads ROM_data outputs from disk
- selects test parameters and reference full-order solutions
- runs ROM solvers and reconstructs full fields
- computes relative error and time ratio metrics
- supports hyper-reduced solvers based on DEIM and ECSW

#### Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name            | Type          | Description                                             |
|-----------------|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| V_sel           | ndarray       | Basis matrix used for reconstruction.                   |
| n_sel           | int           | Mode count used by the ROM.                             |
| param_list_test | array_like    | Test parameter samples.                                 |
| rom_error       | list of float | Relative error values in percent for each test case.    |
| speed_up        | list of float | Time ratios computed as (full-order time) / (ROM time). |

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run_hyper_rom_simulation_deim](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_hyper_rom_simulation_deim) | Run DEIM hyper-ROM evaluation on the test set. |
| [run_hyper_rom_simulation_ecsw](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_hyper_rom_simulation_ecsw) | Run ECSW hyper-ROM evaluation on the test set. |
| [run_rom_simulation](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_rom_simulation) | Run ROM evaluation on the test set. |

##### run_hyper_rom_simulation_deim { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_hyper_rom_simulation_deim }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_hyper_rom_simulation_deim(
    z,
    deim_mat,
    sampled_rows,
)
```

Run DEIM hyper-ROM evaluation on the test set.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name         | Type              | Description                                     | Default    |
|--------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------|------------|
| z            | array_like        | Weight vector stored on the instance.           | _required_ |
| deim_mat     | ndarray           | Interpolation matrix used by the DEIM workflow. | _required_ |
| sampled_rows | array_like of int | Indices of sampled degrees of freedom.          | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name            | Type          | Description                       |
|-----------------|---------------|-----------------------------------|
| hyper_rom_error | list of float | Relative error values in percent. |
| hyper_speed_up  | list of float | Time ratios for each test case.   |

##### run_hyper_rom_simulation_ecsw { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_hyper_rom_simulation_ecsw }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_hyper_rom_simulation_ecsw(
    z,
)
```

Run ECSW hyper-ROM evaluation on the test set.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type       | Description                                    | Default    |
|--------|------------|------------------------------------------------|------------|
| z      | array_like | Element weight vector used by the ECSW solver. | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name            | Type          | Description                       |
|-----------------|---------------|-----------------------------------|
| hyper_rom_error | list of float | Relative error values in percent. |
| hyper_speed_up  | list of float | Time ratios for each test case.   |

##### run_rom_simulation { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_rom_simulation }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.rom_simulation.run_rom_simulation()
```

Run ROM evaluation on the test set.

The method:
- runs the reduced solver for each test parameter
- reconstructs a full field from reduced coordinates
- computes relative error in percent
- computes time ratio (full-order time) / (ROM time)

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name      | Type          | Description                       |
|-----------|---------------|-----------------------------------|
| rom_error | list of float | Relative error values in percent. |
| speed_up  | list of float | Time ratios for each test case.   |

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [assign_properties](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.assign_properties) | Collect callable handles from a problem instance. |
| [get_problem](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.get_problem) | Instantiate a registered problem class. |
| [register_problem](#skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.register_problem) | Register a problem class under a string key. |

### assign_properties { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.assign_properties }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.assign_properties(prob)
```

Collect callable handles from a problem instance.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type    | Description       | Default    |
|--------|---------|-------------------|------------|
| prob   | Problem | Problem instance. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name       | Type   | Description                                                                                                                               |
|------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| properties | tuple  | Tuple with: (parameters, bilinear_forms, linear_forms, domain, properties, fom_solver, rom_solver, hyper_deim_solver, hyper_ecsw_solver). |

### get_problem { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.get_problem }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.get_problem(name)
```

Instantiate a registered problem class.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                | Default    |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------------|------------|
| name   | str    | Registry key used in ``PROBLEM_REGISTRY``. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name             | Type    | Description                       |
|------------------|---------|-----------------------------------|
| problem_instance | Problem | Instance of the registered class. |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type       | Description                                 |
|--------|------------|---------------------------------------------|
|        | ValueError | If ``name`` is not present in the registry. |

### register_problem { #skrom.problem_classes.static.master_class.register_problem }

```python
problem_classes.static.master_class.register_problem(name)
```

Register a problem class under a string key.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                         | Default    |
|--------|--------|-------------------------------------|------------|
| name   | str    | Registry key for the problem class. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type     | Description                                                           |
|--------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| deco   | callable | Decorator that adds the class to ``PROBLEM_REGISTRY`` and returns it. |

#### Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

The registry supports dynamic selection of a problem class based on the
working directory name.
